**PART 19**

**FINANCE & ACCOUNTS**

**Role of Finance and Accounts in a corporate**  
Finance and Accounts (F\&A) has many functions within an organization, and there are many job titles to reflect the varied job responsibilities. Controllers are in charge of financial reporting and the oversight of the accounting activities necessary to develop those reports. Controllers are concerned with payroll functions, accounts receivable, and accounts payable including taxes, inventory control, banking activities, cash flows, loans and investments, audits and any number of short-term asset and liability tracking and monitoring activities. They aid internal and external auditors and are responsible for monitoring and implementing the day-to-day financial operations of the firm.

In most organizations, the **treasurer** might assume many of the duties of the controller. However, the treasurer is also responsible for monitoring cash flow at a firm and frequently is the contact person for bankers, underwriters, and other outside sources of financing. A treasurer may be responsible for structuring loan and debt obligations and determining when and from whom to borrow funds. Treasurers are also responsible for investing excess funds. Where a controller may face inward toward the organization, the treasurer often faces outward as a representative to the public.

The **chief financial officer**, or **CFO**, is in a “big picture” position. The CFO sets policy for working capital management, determines optimal capital structure for the firm, and makes the final decision in matters of Financial planning, forecasting and capital budgeting. 

Monitoring all parameters of F\&A for each project, each BU, each SBU, each customer, each vendor and the Head Office adds complexity to all these processes but provides a financial view of all business units separately.

Every organisation works for revenues from within which it earns its profits.  The vision of an organisation may be different from one another but the final financial goals are the same, ie. Generate Revenues and earn profits. The F\&A may not be directly responsible for the revenues and profits of the company but their monitoring, controlling and strategising all financial activities  indirectly supports the financial goals of an organization. Therefore the role of F\&A cannot be over emphasized.  

**CHAPTER : CROSS ALLOTTMENT OF SBU FUNDS**

Each SBU in an organisation is engaged with a particular kind of business. There may be many SBUs in an organisation. Sometimes it so happens that a particular SBU is doing well financially whereas another SBU is not performing well, rather incurring losses or earling revenues late due to client payment terms. In such a case, the CFO then tries to transfer funds from the SBUs earning well and receiving payments from clients on time to the SBUs incurring losses. By such actions, the Director ruins the business of the SBUs who were performing well. I will explain how.

Assume C1 is the direct cost of materials and services in a project and C2 is the indirect cost, ie. the project management costs including site management costs and overhead costs of the company; then the total cost of the project, say C \= C1 \+ C2. Now a company builds up a net profit, say 7% to build the price. So price P \= 1.07 C. For example if Rs 100 is the total cost of a particular item, the corresponding price is Rs 107 quoted to the client. Remember, in the price of Rs 107, Rs 100 is the cost which is 93.45% of the price (100/107 \= 93.45%).

Assume that the standard client payment terms are (1) 10% Advance for mobilisation against a Advance Bank Guarantee of like amount (2) 80% progressive payment against submission of bills for works done and (3) Retention money of 10% which may be released on completion of the project against a Bank Guarantee of like amount valid till the end of Defects Liability Period (DLP).

From above terms, when you bill for a work done worth for say Rs 100, then the following are the mathematics you should remember:

Work done worth \= Rs 100 (billed to client)  
Pro rata advance already received before commencement of work \= Rs 10 (10% advance)  
Retention payment \= Rs 10 (10% retention), to be received after completion of the project, as explained above  
Progressive payment received \= Rs 8 (80% progressive payment)  
Total money received \= Rs 10 (Advance) \+ Rs 80 (progressive) \= Rs 90

New documents as required from insurance companies from time to time are submitted as and when required but there is no hurry for the Insurance Manager to receive the claims. This is very astonishing.
  3671→
  3672→Insurance Managers must be sensitised that claims filed are because the project have undergone losses, yet the Manager has no concern. Sometimes it may happen that the claims are itself the profits for the project, in other words, the project will incur losses if the claims are not received. 
  3673→
  3674→Therefore, every claim must have a set time target and the performance of the manager has to be evaluated with respect to fulfilling these time targets. Losses of a project or diminishing profits must be arrested through these claims. The claims received shall be far more in value than paying salaries for a dedicated manager for the same. Hence claims claim an important parameter in project profitability.
  3675→
  3676→**The salient points of are:**
  3677→
  3678→1. Insurance claims must be filed promptly as soon as losses or theft is reported from the site.  
  3679→2. Every insurance claim must have a time target and the Insurance Manager be appraised accordingly  
  3680→3. Receipt of insurance claims can offset the losses of a project or improve its profitability.  
  3681→
  3682→**SOME GOOD PRACTICES**

  3684→**Listing progress of Insurance claims**  
  3685→Receiving insurance claims is a lengthy process. Documentary proof is the essential part in claiming insurances. Insurance claims may pertain to different projects that an organization is executing. Also, there may be multiple insurance claims against the same project. Again, many claims may have been filed with different insurance agencies. The Insurance Manager must be very active to interact with all the agencies, submit further documents as and when required and expedite claims for the organization.

file.content:
**PART 23**

**INSURANCE**  
	  
**Role of Insurance in a corporate**  
Insurance plays an important role in an organization by protecting against various risks that could disrupt business operations. Insurance ensures financial stability while mitigating risks. Comprehensive insurance policies like life and health insurance also attracts talent. Some legal compliance like mandatory third party insurance for cars is met by taking insurances.

You must be aware of Car Insurance. If you are an owner of a bike or a car, you know that car insurance is mandatory if you own a car or a bike. The insurance can be a Third  party insurance covering loss or damage to third parties which is mandatory while comprehensive insurance which covers all the cars for bumper to bumper damage and all the individuals involved in the accident including yourself is optional in India. However, Personal Accident Insurance, known as PA cover, is also mandatory as per law in India.

We also have house insurance covering loss or damage to house property and its contents, life insurance covering financial benefits to your nominee in case of your death, health insurance covering medical expenses of yourself and your family, EMI insurance covering waiver of EMIs in case of your death, cybercrime insurance in case you are cheated or extorted by cyber criminals.  
	  
You may observe that all the above kinds of insurances are covers to different risks. And the premium is a cost incurred to cover the risk. 

Similar to above insurances, you can also take a Project insurance covering the losses or damage to a Project due to unforeseen circumstances. Coupled with this, you have to take a Workmen Compensation Policy (WCP) to cover lives of workmen and vehicle insurances for all the vehicles attached to the project or elsewhere. WCP has to be mandatorily taken for any project in India.   

**CHAPTER : FALSE CLAIMS**

Some managers in the Insurance department tend to claim higher amounts for project losses than actually the losses are. They feel that the surveyor from the insurance company will find out some discrepancies and strike some portion of their claims, hence claim higher. In my opinion, such an act is unethical. And if the claims are genuine, then it is also unethical on the part of the surveyor to strike some portion of the claims. I have observed that employees unethically submit higher claims in fear and anticipation that some portion of the claims will be struck off by the surveyor.  

Such an unfair practice on the part of the employee as well as on the part of the surveyor is unwarranted. Employees should resort to filing correct claims with proof and surveyors should okay claims wherever they are substantiated with proof.

**The salient points of are:**

1. Insurance department should not resort to false claims of higher amounts than the actual losses.  
2. Insurance department should convince the surveyors that their claims are genuine and should not be opposed.  
   

**CHAPTER : NO TARGETS**

I have observed that the Insurance manager has several claims filed for different projects in the past but have no time targets to receive them. New documents as required from insurance companies from time to time are submitted as and when required but there is no hurry for the Insurance Manager to receive the claims. This is very astonishing.

Insurance Managers must be sensitised that claims filed are because the project have undergone losses, yet the Manager has no concern. Sometimes it may happen that the claims are itself the profits for the project, in other words, the project will incur losses if the claims are not received. 

Therefore, every claim must have a set time target and the performance of the manager has to be evaluated with respect to fulfilling these time targets. Losses of a project or diminishing profits must be arrested through these claims. The claims received shall be far more in value than paying salaries for a dedicated manager for the same. Hence claims claim an important parameter in project profitability.

**The salient points of are:**

1. Insurance claims must be filed promptly as soon as losses or theft is reported from the site.  
2. Every insurance claim must have a time target and the Insurance Manager be appraised accordingly  
3. Receipt of insurance claims can offset the losses of a project or improve its profitability.  

**SOME GOOD PRACTICES**

**Listing progress of Insurance claims**  
Receiving insurance claims is a lengthy process. Documentary proof is the essential part in claiming insurances. Insurance claims may pertain to different projects that an organization is executing. Also, there may be multiple insurance claims against the same project. Again, many claims may have been filed with different insurance agencies. The Insurance Manager must be very active to interact with all the agencies, submit further documents as and when required and expedite claims for the organization.

file.content:
Similarly, the concept can be extended to the positions associated with each managerial grade. However, framework for positions can be a challenge because a small sized group company will also be eager to keep a position like the Vice President (feel good factor) just as a large sized group company would also like to do so. But you can easily imagine that there is a lot of difference in the status and strength of a Vice President of a large sized company versus the Vice President of a small sized company. However, at least, the framework for the Managerial grades can be kept the same.

Now, in the Managerial grades from M1 to M20, the rules pertaining to Leaves, Benefits and facilities extended, Local, Domestic and Foreign travels, etc can also be tagged to each Managerial grade with different applicable data. Some facility like Foreign Travel may not be applicable to a particular or range of managerial grades.

The above concept can also be extended to the Staff grade.

There may be challenges in adopting such a common framework of grades across group companies but the advantage of it is its uniformity. After all, every option has pros and cons; still we need to adopt a particular option in life if the positives of the option weigh more.  

**ANNEXURES**

**PART 28 : ANNEXURES**

Annexures deals with subjects which are sometimes wrongly interpreted or less understood. Therefore I thought to discuss on some subjects in this Part to provide clarity to young professionals. However, you can delve on these subjects in various documents for a deeper understanding.

**ANNEXURE: VISION & MISSION**

Many employees are not clear what a Mission or a Vision of an organization is. Let us try to understand.

Every organisation has a Mission and a Vision and they emphasize this to its employees and the outside world through a statement called the Vision and Mission statement.

3282:**CHAPTER : NOT BACK TO BACK TERMS**

3070→**The salient points are:**
  3071→
  3072→1. Do not keep too many consultants for similar work. They may have contradicting ideas and approaches leading to confusion amongst employees  
  3073→2. The Head must discuss with consultants to have uniformity in their approaches  
  3074→3. Different approaches by different consultants for similar work may be taken with justified reasons and approved by the Head  
  3075→4. Many consultants of contractors recruited after retirement from their previous customer/client organisations fail to perform since they now sit on opposite chairs  
  3076→
  3077→**CHAPTER : NON EMPOWERMENT OF WOMEN**
  3078→
  3079→There can be no discrimination when it comes to appraisal of women employees. Some HR have a fear to elevate women to top positions as if such positions could be held by the men folk only\! Fear of having women controlling them\! I fail to understand why they have such a low mentality and in what kind of society and environment they have been brought up. First and foremost, action by the Top Leader is to get rid of such HR guys.
  3080→
  3081→Women have shown tremendous power and talent to perform, and in many occasions much better than the men folk. They have become the best doctors, best scientists, best engineers, best sportswomen, best drivers, best politicians, best pilots, best athletes, best military personnel and best in many other different professions.
  3082→
  3083→Let me give some examples. Amongst Indian politicians, Indira Gandhi, Sonia Gandhi, Jayalalithaa, Mayawati, Mamata Banerjee, Sushma Swaraj, Vasundhara Raje, Sheila Dikshit, Smriti Irani, Ambika Soni, Supriya Sule, Agatha Sangma are/were renowned politicians. Irrespective of who was good or bad in your opinion, they worked hard while at top positions. Similarly, in the Hindi musical arena, Lata Mangeswar, Alka Yagnik, Neha Kakkar, Shreya Ghoshal, Sunidhi Chouhan, Palak Muchhal, Tulsi Kumar, Neeti Mohan, Monali Thakur, Richa Sharma have been successful singers of their times. Again, irrespective of whose songs you like or don’t, they have risen to great heights. I can go on and on for other diversified fields where women have risen to great heights. In the field of women cricket, women have commenced playin in ICC World Cup Cricket and they have even won the 2025 World Cricket Trophy. Such are the performances of women today. This is only to prove to the men folk that women are equally talented to take the top positions. CHRO, please note\!
  3084→
  3085→Actually, it may be true that women are generally physically weaker than the men folk, but that has got nothing to do with the best professions you are in as cited above. Leaders do not require huge muscle strength\! After all, they are not body builders\!
  3086→
  3087→Oh\! Suddenly I remember about a humorous incident when I mentioned about the best pilots. In one my flight from Delhi to Mumbai, I was accompanied by my boss. The pilot announced their names before the takeoff and we understood that it was an all-women driven flight. My boss suddenly became very nervous. He said “I am feeling afraid; I understand that both the pilots on board are women, and so are all the air hostesses, will they be able to fly such a huge aircraft”. I tried to explain him that sir, the aircraft is flown not by strength but by skills, you don’t need huge muscles to steer an aircraft. The digital buttons can fly the aircrafts. He smiled out of shame but he kept his eyes closed throughout the journey. In fact, I was very appreciative of the pilots because I experienced one of the smoothest takeoff and landing. My boss later understood that flying a plane is not about physical strength, it’s about your flying skills. In future, he never bothered who the pilot was\! The essence of this story is that women can also be the best pilots on earth.
  3088→
  3089→So, why do some people have wrong conceptions about capabilities of women? But then, some raise questions on reservations of women in various institutions, queues, etc. Probably this is to encourage upliftment of women so that they come up with more numbers in a male dominated society. To sum it up, HR must encourage women in top positions and reserve some positions for them if need be. 
  3090→
  3091→**The salient points are:**
  3092→
  3093→1. Today women have been the best performers in every sphere of life  
  3094→2. More and more women must be given chance to assume top leadership positions provided they are proficient in Strategy and Vision  
  3095→3. There should be no shame to work under women leaders in a male dominated society  
  3096→4. With upliftment of women in every field, one day may hopefully come when the society will be dominated equally by men and women
  3097→
  3098→ 
  3099→
  3100→**CHAPTER : THE TOP HEAVY BURDEN**
  3101→
  3102→Refer to the Chapter where Employee overheads have been discussed. You have seen that Employee Overheads \= Total CTC/Total Turnover. This could range from 1% to as high as 7% depending on the type of industry.
  3103→
  3104→There may be many grades or positions in an organisation for officers, like Asst. Manager, Manager, Deputy Manager, Senior Manager, General Manager, Asst. Vice President, Vice President, Executive Vice President, President, and so on and so forth. All these positions can be clubbed into three levels : 1\. Junior Management; 2\. Middle Management; 3 Top Management. Other than these management levels, there would be supporting staff in the organisations such as the receptionist, office help, photocopy  personnel, drivers, sweepers, pantry boys and girls, etc. All the permanent officers and permanent staff will contribute to the total employee cost to the company, that is, the total CTC

**CHAPTER : NON EMPOWERMENT OF WOMEN**

There can be no discrimination when it comes to appraisal of women employees. Some HR have a fear to elevate women to top positions as if such positions could be held by the men folk only\! Women have shown tremendous power and talent to perform, and in many occasions much better than the men folk. They have become the best doctors, best scientists, best engineers, best sportswomen, best drivers, best politicians, best pilots, best athletes, best military personnel and best in many other different professions.

Let me give some examples. Amongst Indian politicians, Indira Gandhi, Sonia Gandhi, Jayalalithaa, Mayawati, Mamata Banerjee, Sushma Swaraj, Vasundhara Raje, Sheila Dikshit, Smriti Irani, Ambika Soni, Supriya Sule, Agatha Sangma are/were renowned politicians. Actually, it may be true that women are generally physically weaker than the men folk, but that has got nothing to do with the best professions you are in as cited above. Leaders do not require huge muscle strength\! After all, they are not body builders\!

Oh\! Suddenly I remember about a humorous incident when I mentioned about the best pilots. In one my flight from Delhi to Mumbai, I was accompanied by my boss. The pilot announced their names before the takeoff and we understood that it was an all-women driven flight. My boss suddenly became very nervous. He said “I am feeling afraid; I understand that both the pilots on board are women, and so are all the air hostesses, will they be able to fly such a huge aircraft”. I tried to explain him that sir, the aircraft is flown not by strength but by skills, you don’t need huge muscles to steer an aircraft. The digital buttons can fly the aircrafts. He smiled out of shame but he kept his eyes closed throughout the journey. In fact, I was very appreciative of the pilots because I experienced one of the smoothest takeoff and landing. **CHAPTER : THE TOP HEAVY BURDEN**

Vice President, Vice President, Executive Vice President, President, and so on and so forth. All these positions can be clubbed into three levels : 1\. Junior Management; 2\. Other than these management levels, there would be supporting staff in the organisations such as the receptionist, office help, photocopy  personnel, drivers, sweepers, pantry boys and girls, etc. All the permanent officers and permanent staff will contribute to the total employee cost to the company, that is, the total CTC

content.content:
4471→**INSURANCE**
  4472→
  4473→Insurance is a cover for an individual to safeguard his interest. For example life insurance will safeguard against the livelihood costs of the nominee and his family in case of death, health insurance will safeguard against the medical costs for treatment, property insurance will safeguard against EMI payments by the nominee in case of death, house insurance will safeguard against the costs for damage of house due to calamities, cyber insurance will safeguard against cyber frauds and threats.
  4474→
  4475→You have to pay premiums for taking such insurances. But many wise people argue foolishly at the end of a year that they have been profitable by not taking insurance cover and thus paying premiums since nothing happened to them in the year. Financially it may be so but technically not. No one desires that something bad happens to them ever; however, one should think that you need to de-risk such undesirable events. Hence is the importance of insurance. All individuals are advised to take insurances. 
  4476→
  4477→**Insurance is a cover for risk; the premium is for attaining peace of mind.**  
  4478→       
  4479→**HEALTH**
  4480→
  4481→Health is wealth. We understand it better as we age and we sometimes find ourselves in unhealthy conditions or become sick. While we are young, being sick is rare and so we then think Money is Wealth, not Health. However, today many youngsters are prone to sickness due to many factors, personal and environmental.
  4482→
  4483→As we remain busy in our corporate life, health, the most precious asset that one possess, is often ignored. Continuous such ignorance may lead to health complications later on. One must sincerely take care of his health while on service. Do not postpone it till retirement. Maintain health right from day one.
  4484→
  4485→Health pertains to different domains of an individual;  
  4486→Physical health is about taking care of your body;  
  4487→Mental health is about taking care of your mind;  
  4488→Spiritual health is about taking care of your soul;
  4489→
  4490→Body, mind and soul are all interrelated and define your wellbeing. Your satisfactory wellbeing is a catalyst to a good personal or corporate life. If your body or the mind or the soul or any combination thereof is disturbed, your personal life and corporate life too will be disturbed.
  4491→
  4492→Health pertaining to other domains of an individual is:  
  4493→Financial health is about managing your finances and assets  
  4494→Sexual health is about taking interest in your sex life  
  4495→Social health is about your involvement in the society  
  4496→Job health is about your satisfaction in your job or business
  4497→
  4498→Above health parameters too affect your wellbeing. For example, if your financial health is poor, you will be anxious and will hamper your mental health. And if your mental health is poor, your physical health will be affected. All are interrelated.
  4499→
  4500→Also, don’t miss your annual or bi-annual basic health check-ups comprising of blood tests, Chest x-ray, USG and ECG. It is interesting to note that many people think they are fine and healthy but health check-ups reveal that certain health parameters are beyond normal values which require immediate treatment. What I am trying to sensitise that many such illness develop within our bodies SILENTLY.  For example, thousands of people do not know that they are pre-diabetic or have high blood pressure because they don’t check. These are silent killers. So please don’t miss your health check-ups.    
  4501→**Health is wealth. It is one of the most precious assets that one possesses. Please don’t ignore**  
  4502→   
  4503→**PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT**
  4504→
  4505→This is again a ignored subject while we are in service. Someone comes and convinces of investing in a FD, we do it. Someone comes and asks to invest in ULIPs, we do it. Someone else comes and convinces us that there is lot of money to be earned in the stock market, we invest in the equities. Someone says to buy a flat instead of paying rents, we do so. That someone could be you yourself. You may think to put some money in gold, you do it. Whatever someone tries to convince or we are convinced about investment, we do it. However, all these investments are done in a haphazard manner.
  4506→
  4507→Here comes the issue of portfolio management. There is no harm in investing money in different class of assets, but it has to be done in a scientific manner. Firstly, the class of assets can be classified into equity, debt, property and metal. The percentage of investment into these different class could be based on one's age (the less the age, the more may be invested in equities), risk taking power (the less the risk taking power is, the more may be invested in debts), interest (if on a transferable job, someone may not be interested in property). 
  4508→
  4509→A typical mid aged person with moderate risk taking power can invest like;  
  4510→35% on equity  
  4511→25% on debt  
  4512→25% on property  
  4513→15% on metal
  4514→
  4515→Equity could be the stock market or equity MFs. Debt could be bank FD, corporate FD and corporate bonds. Property could be residential or commercial. Metal could be physical gold or silver, paper gold or silver, platinum or copper, zinc and aluminum in the MCX stock exchange. 
  4516→
  4517→Please take the help of a portfolio manager, who could be your Finance guy himself, to guide you for a good portfolio.
  4518→
  4519→**Invest wisely to maintain a good portfolio which will lead to earning good profits**
  4520→
  4521→**WORK-LIFE BALANCE**
  4522→
  4523→This is a hot debate often happening even on TV debates. How many live s have God given you? One, I suppose. Therefore you are never going to get the time lapsed of your life. Even the richest man on earth cannot buy time which has lapsed. Time gone is gone forever. And your past is also gone forever.
  4524→
  4525→So, while we have to struggle very hard in the corporates to do well in life and rise up the corporate ladder, we also need to give time for yourself (me time) and your family and friends. All is not work in life. We definitely have to work; it is a foundation to have a great life with our family and friends. So you need to balance between work and life and that is what is called the Work-Life balance. If you provide all the time to work, your relationship with your family may not be so intimate and loving, you may lose your friends and you may end up leaving all your hobbies that you so much loved to pursue.
  4526→
  4527→For a few days the balance may be upset due to pressure of work or on family matters, but on an overall basis you must strike a balance between work and life.
  4528→
  4529→**WORK-LIFE balance is about providing time to office as well as to our near and dear ones.**
  4530→
  4531→**GRATEFULNESS**
  4532→
  4533→Why do we touch the feet of our parents when we meet them? Why do we touch the feet of our beloved school teachers or our coaches? Because we are grateful to them for the care, love and teachings they have imparted to us. Without their care and support, we would not have been what we are; therefore they have been large contributors to our success in life.
  4534→
  4535→However, very unfortunately, I have seen many people not being grateful to their contributors once the contributor is no more in a position to contribute to you anymore. For example, as a business Head, you might have taught the intricacies of business to your subordinate and encouraged him and promoted him to assume higher responsibilities which he was capable of, but as you retire and you are no more in a position to give good bonus or promote him any further you are simply forgotten. It is often said that in a street or a market a clerk or a staff do not even look at a Director who have retired from his organization because the clerk knows that the Director now cannot give him any benefits further. These people are so selfish because they think why they should waste time, energy and money to talk or meet their bosses when they are useless for them\!  
  4536→
  4537→Above kind of incidences happen in every sphere of life, even within our relatives and “so called” friends. So why should someone be grateful. It has been proven scientifically they gratefulness leads to:
  4538→
  4539→1) Emotional well-being: Gratitude fosters positive emotions, reduces stress. Expressing appreciation leads to increased happiness and satisfaction of the soul  
  4540→2) Physical well-being: Gratitude improves physical health by promoting better sleep, reduces blood pressure and enhances immunity  
  4541→3) Mental well-being: Gratitude lowers depression and anxiety and helps in facing challenges in life  
  4542→4) Social connections: Gratitude strengthens relationships and reduces loneliness  
  4543→5) Contentment: Gratitude leads to greater contentment. It shifts focus from what one lacks to what one has promoting a sense of fulfillment
  4544→
  4545→**Practice gratefulness; you may not be aware how it improves your well-being**
  4546→
  4547→**WEIGH YOUR OPTIONS**
  4548→
  4549→I find it frustrating when one tries to figure out only the negative sides of a person, an option or an entity. Be it yourself, or a singer, or an actor, or a politician, or a team, or a political party, or a school, or whatever, there can be good things as well as bad things about it. Any human or agency or entity cannot be perfect in all parameters. But a good human or an agency or an entity has presumably many more good things than the bad things about them. You need to appreciate those good things and not ignore and focus on bad things. You can also weigh the importance of the good things and the bad things and come to a conclusion if the entity is good or bad.
  4550→
  4551→In our personal life and also in the corporate, we sometimes have many options to select from. You must weigh the positive and negative factors of each of the options and come to a conclusion on which option to choose. It is obvious that all options will have some negative points. There are people who are not happy with any option because he tries to see only the negative side of each option. These people cannot make proper and fast decisions needed in the corporate world. You have to choose an option with the greatest advantages amidst some disadvantages that it may have. 
  4552→
  4553→**SABJANTALOGY**
  4554→
  4555→This is the name of a subject which I coined after coming across some people who fit into this class of people. Just like Biology, Physiology, Oncology, Sociology, Anthropology, etc., Sabjantalogy deals with the science of knowing everything on earth.
  4556→
  4557→Some people may advise you on how to cook better, advise you on better medicines, teach you how to play a sports better, seemingly knows about any kind of industry on earth, knows about fabric and garments, gives you lecture on politics, or even on rocket science. The most astonishing part is that all these “knowhow??” has been formed on his own whims and fancies and not formed by reading books or learning from somewhere.
  4558→
  4559→Say for example, if you are an Arts graduate and have formed an idea about a medicine with your own experiment without consulting a doctor or learning it from any medical journal or reading about it from newspaper or elsewhere, is it right for you to prescribe the medicine to your friend? There can be hundreds of examples like this on various subjects.
  4560→
  4561→Today the world has lot of expertise in every field of life. If you really want to prescribe a medicine, ask the doctor, or if you really want to advise on how to make the best cakes, ten consult youtube or a chef. Why provide your “so called” knowledge which is actually wrong.
  4562→
  4563→**Don’t be a Sabtanjalogist; ask the specialist or learn about it.**  
  4564→
  4565→**DON’T BE A MISER**
  4566→
  4567→What is the purpose of earning money; to spend it wisely and reasonably; Isn’t it? But some accumulate money for their own satisfaction and not spend them for reasons not known to them. They are misers. Misers are of two types: universal misers and selfish misers. Universal misers are those who are reluctant to spend for anyone on earth. Selfish misers are those who are willing to spend only for him and family but not beyond.
  4568→
  4569→Spending must be wise and reasonable; you need to keep reasonable amount of money for medical emergencies, education and marriage of your children and for all mandatory purposes of life. Beyond that, you should spend for yourself, family and others for things that you love in life, be it for food, movies, clothes, gadget, tours and travels, hobbies or whatever that you think will be useful or make someone happy. Remember, there is no concept of Fixed Deposits in heaven. Everything that you earn in life has to be left. We have been born naked and will depart to Heaven naked. It is one life that God has given us. Enjoy it to the fullest extent. 
  4570→
  4571→How much to save, how much to invest and how much to spend may vary between individuals, but they have to be wisely and reasonably thought of practically. Allot money that you earn for savings, investment and expenses in a balanced practical manner.
  4572→
  4573→**Save & invest what is necessary. But spend too to enjoy and live your life.** 
  4574→
  4575→**SPEND TIME WITH YOUR CHILD**
  4576→
  4577→There is a joke which you will hear while your child grows up. When you are asked how tall your child has grown up, you answer by gesture with your hands apart horizontally. This is because your child leaves for school early while you are asleep and the child is asleep when you come home late from office. Therefore you have not seen him grow tall vertically on office days. Suddenly you can see him how tall he is when you are at home on Sundays. 
  4578→
  4579→However, do not take it as a simple joke. Once your child is 18 years old and pass out Class XII, he may be going out of station or abroad for higher studies and subsequently for good jobs anywhere on this earth. This means you may get to stay with your child at your home only for the first 18 years of his life and thereafter you will miss him except for a few days when the child will have time to meet you at home on his holidays.
  4580→
  4581→At the same time, you will be very busy at office trying to secure your good future year after year. But amidst this rat race, do not ignore to spend as much time as possible with your child. Remember you may not get to see him for long after he reaches 18 years of age. Your good times spent with your child will remain etched in your mind forever. Do not miss these times. It will never come back again 
  4582→
  4583→**CULTIVATE A HOBBY**
  4584→
  4585→There will be times in your life when you are very sad. Maybe your boss did not give you a promotion which you thought of sure to get, or could be your fiancé decided for a break up, or incidents like these. You would not like to be besides of your friends or family at such sad moments. You would prefer to be alone for some while. So how do you spend these times alone? Cultivate a hobby which will keep you happy and busy to tide over your sad times or after your retirement. You can learn to play cards on your own; you can learn to play an instrument; you can learn to sing; you can learn to paint; or any other hobby that will give you a sense of fulfillment.
  4586→
  4587→Music was my greatest de-stressor. Playing the harmonica and the guitar lit up my soul at sad times. These instruments are still a part of my life after retirement. 
  4588→
  4589→**DEMONSTRATE UNBIASED PROFESIONALISM**
  4590→
  4591→I have read it everywhere that even a peon would not like to wish or see your face on the street after you have retired as a Director or from a big position in the corporate. This may be true but not always. 
  4592→
  4593→As a leader, if you had shown good leadership by demonstrating correct attitude, being kind and supportive, managing people humanly with care and respect, being grateful to people who supported you, encouraging people to do well and rewarding them for good work and your actions on employees and all stakeholders always being unbiased and transparent and to top it all if you had been successful in your organization, then people, whoever knew you and know you presently hearing from others do remember and respect you even after you retire or even die. 
  4594→
  4595→Being unbiased even with your close colleagues and demonstrating your professionalism against personal bias at all times is seen as a great leadership. Such successful leaders are never forgotten.  
  4596→
  4597→**ENJOY LIFE**
  4598→
  4599→The Almighty have given you ONE life only. Even if your super rich, you have one life only. So always enjoy your life.
  4600→
  4601→How many hours do you have in a year? 24 hours a day x365 days a year \= 8760 hours. Assume you sleep 8 hours a day; therefore you spend 8 x 365 hours \= 2920 hours. Assume you work 10 hours a day (except Sundays and 12 annual holidays) and commute 2 hours a day to office; therefore you spend 12 x (365-52-12) hours \= 3612 hours. So you have (8760-2920-3612) hours, ie. 2228 hours a year excluding time for work and sleep. 2228 hours out of 8760 hours means around 25% of your life; could be less if you work more. Have you ever imagined that you have only 25% of your life free of work and sleep which you can utilize for yourself, family and friends and relatives? file.content:
**INSURANCE**

Insurance is a cover for an individual to safeguard his interest. For example life insurance will safeguard against the livelihood costs of the nominee and his family in case of death, health insurance will safeguard against the medical costs for treatment, property insurance will safeguard against EMI payments by the nominee in case of death, house insurance will safeguard against the costs for damage of house due to calamities, cyber insurance will safeguard against cyber frauds and threats.

You have to pay premiums for taking such insurances. But many wise people argue foolishly at the end of a year that they have been profitable by not taking insurance cover and thus paying premiums since nothing happened to them in the year. Financially it may be so but technically not. No one desires that something bad happens to them ever; however, one should think that you need to de-risk such undesirable events. Hence is the importance of insurance. All individuals are advised to take insurances. 

**Insurance is a cover for risk; the premium is for attaining peace of mind.**  
       
**HEALTH**

Health is wealth. We understand it better as we age and we sometimes find ourselves in unhealthy conditions or become sick. While we are young, being sick is rare and so we then think Money is Wealth, not Health. However, today many youngsters are prone to sickness due to many factors, personal and environmental.

As we remain busy in our corporate life, health, the most precious asset that one possess, is often ignored. Continuous such ignorance may lead to health complications later on. One must sincerely take care of his health while on service. Do not postpone it till retirement. Maintain health right from day one.

Health pertains to different domains of an individual;  
Physical health is about taking care of your body;  
Mental health is about taking care of your mind;  
Spiritual health is about taking care of your soul;

Body, mind and soul are all interrelated and define your wellbeing. Your satisfactory wellbeing is a catalyst to a good personal or corporate life. If your body or the mind or the soul or any combination thereof is disturbed, your personal life and corporate life too will be disturbed.

Health pertaining to other domains of an individual is:  
Financial health is about managing your finances and assets  
Sexual health is about taking interest in your sex life  
Social health is about your involvement in the society  
Job health is about your satisfaction in your job or business

Above health parameters too affect your wellbeing. For example, if your financial health is poor, you will be anxious and will hamper your mental health. And if your mental health is poor, your physical health will be affected. All are interrelated.

Also, don’t miss your annual or bi-annual basic health check-ups comprising of blood tests, Chest x-ray, USG and ECG. It is interesting to note that many people think they are fine and healthy but health check-ups reveal that certain health parameters are beyond normal values which require immediate treatment. What I am trying to sensitise that many such illness develop within our bodies SILENTLY.  For example, thousands of people do not know that they are pre-diabetic or have high blood pressure because they don’t check. These are silent killers. So please don’t miss your health check-ups.    
**Health is wealth. It is one of the most precious assets that one possesses. Please don’t ignore**  
   
**PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT**

This is again a ignored subject while we are in service. Someone comes and convinces of investing in a FD, we do it. Someone comes and asks to invest in ULIPs, we do it. Someone else comes and convinces us that there is lot of money to be earned in the stock market, we invest in the equities. Someone says to buy a flat instead of paying rents, we do so. That someone could be you yourself. You may think to put some money in gold, you do it. Whatever someone tries to convince or we are convinced about investment, we do it. However, all these investments are done in a haphazard manner.

Here comes the issue of portfolio management. There is no harm in investing money in different class of assets, but it has to be done in a scientific manner. Firstly, the class of assets can be classified into equity, debt, property and metal. The percentage of investment into these different class could be based on one's age (the less the age, the more may be invested in equities), risk taking power (the less the risk taking power is, the more may be invested in debts), interest (if on a transferable job, someone may not be interested in property). 

A typical mid aged person with moderate risk taking power can invest like;  
35% on equity  
25% on debt  
25% on property  
15% on metal

Equity could be the stock market or equity MFs. Debt could be bank FD, corporate FD and corporate bonds. Property could be residential or commercial. Metal could be physical gold or silver, paper gold or silver, platinum or copper, zinc and aluminum in the MCX stock exchange. 

Please take the help of a portfolio manager, who could be your Finance guy himself, to guide you for a good portfolio.

**Invest wisely to maintain a good portfolio which will lead to earning good profits**

**WORK-LIFE BALANCE**

This is a hot debate often happening even on TV debates. How many live s have God given you? One, I suppose. Therefore you are never going to get the time lapsed of your life. Even the richest man on earth cannot buy time which has lapsed. Time gone is gone forever. And your past is also gone forever.

So, while we have to struggle very hard in the corporates to do well in life and rise up the corporate ladder, we also need to give time for yourself (me time) and your family and friends. All is not work in life. We definitely have to work; it is a foundation to have a great life with our family and friends. So you need to balance between work and life and that is what is called the Work-Life balance. If you provide all the time to work, your relationship with your family may not be so intimate and loving, you may lose your friends and you may end up leaving all your hobbies that you so much loved to pursue.

For a few days the balance may be upset due to pressure of work or on family matters, but on an overall basis you must strike a balance between work and life.

**WORK-LIFE balance is about providing time to office as well as to our near and dear ones.**

**GRATEFULNESS**

Why do we touch the feet of our parents when we meet them? Why do we touch the feet of our beloved school teachers or our coaches? Because we are grateful to them for the care, love and teachings they have imparted to us. Without their care and support, we would not have been what we are; therefore they have been large contributors to our success in life.

However, very unfortunately, I have seen many people not being grateful to their contributors once the contributor is no more in a position to contribute to you anymore. For example, as a business Head, you might have taught the intricacies of business to your subordinate and encouraged him and promoted him to assume higher responsibilities which he was capable of, but as you retire and you are no more in a position to give good bonus or promote him any further you are simply forgotten. It is often said that in a street or a market a clerk or a staff do not even look at a Director who have retired from his organization because the clerk knows that the Director now cannot give him any benefits further. These people are so selfish because they think why they should waste time, energy and money to talk or meet their bosses when they are useless for them\!  

Above kind of incidences happen in every sphere of life, even within our relatives and “so called” friends. So why should someone be grateful. It has been proven scientifically they gratefulness leads to:

1) Emotional well-being: Gratitude fosters positive emotions, reduces stress. Expressing appreciation leads to increased happiness and satisfaction of the soul  
2) Physical well-being: Gratitude improves physical health by promoting better sleep, reduces blood pressure and enhances immunity  
3) Mental well-being: Gratitude lowers depression and anxiety and helps in facing challenges in life  
4) Social connections: Gratitude strengthens relationships and reduces loneliness  
5) Contentment: Gratitude leads to greater contentment. It shifts focus from what one lacks to what one has promoting a sense of fulfillment

**Practice gratefulness; you may not be aware how it improves your well-being**

**WEIGH YOUR OPTIONS**

I find it frustrating when one tries to figure out only the negative sides of a person, an option or an entity. Be it yourself, or a singer, or an actor, or a politician, or a team, or a political party, or a school, or whatever, there can be good things as well as bad things about it. Any human or agency or entity cannot be perfect in all parameters. But a good human or an agency or an entity has presumably many more good things than the bad things about them. You need to appreciate those good things and not ignore and focus on bad things. You can also weigh the importance of the good things and the bad things and come to a conclusion if the entity is good or bad.

In our personal life and also in the corporate, we sometimes have many options to select from. You must weigh the positive and negative factors of each of the options and come to a conclusion on which option to choose. It is obvious that all options will have some negative points. There are people who are not happy with any option because he tries to see only the negative side of each option. These people cannot make proper and fast decisions needed in the corporate world. You have to choose an option with the greatest advantages amidst some disadvantages that it may have. 

**SABJANTALOGY**

This is the name of a subject which I coined after coming across some people who fit into this class of people. Just like Biology, Physiology, Oncology, Sociology, Anthropology, etc., Sabjantalogy deals with the science of knowing everything on earth.

Some people may advise you on how to cook better, advise you on better medicines, teach you how to play a sports better, seemingly knows about any kind of industry on earth, knows about fabric and garments, gives you lecture on politics, or even on rocket science. The most astonishing part is that all these “knowhow??” has been formed on his own whims and fancies and not formed by reading books or learning from somewhere.

Say for example, if you are an Arts graduate and have formed an idea about a medicine with your own experiment without consulting a doctor or learning it from any medical journal or reading about it from newspaper or elsewhere, is it right for you to prescribe the medicine to your friend? There can be hundreds of examples like this on various subjects.

Today the world has lot of expertise in every field of life. If you really want to prescribe a medicine, ask the doctor, or if you really want to advise on how to make the best cakes, ten consult youtube or a chef. Why provide your “so called” knowledge which is actually wrong.

**Don’t be a Sabtanjalogist; ask the specialist or learn about it.**  

**DON’T BE A MISER**

What is the purpose of earning money; to spend it wisely and reasonably; Isn’t it? But some accumulate money for their own satisfaction and not spend them for reasons not known to them. They are misers. Misers are of two types: universal misers and selfish misers. Universal misers are those who are reluctant to spend for anyone on earth. Selfish misers are those who are willing to spend only for him and family but not beyond.

Spending must be wise and reasonable; you need to keep reasonable amount of money for medical emergencies, education and marriage of your children and for all mandatory purposes of life. Beyond that, you should spend for yourself, family and others for things that you love in life, be it for food, movies, clothes, gadget, tours and travels, hobbies or whatever that you think will be useful or make someone happy. Remember, there is no concept of Fixed Deposits in heaven. Everything that you earn in life has to be left. We have been born naked and will depart to Heaven naked. It is one life that God has given us. Enjoy it to the fullest extent. 

How much to save, how much to invest and how much to spend may vary between individuals, but they have to be wisely and reasonably thought of practically. Allot money that you earn for savings, investment and expenses in a balanced practical manner.

**Save & invest what is necessary. But spend too to enjoy and live your life.** 

**SPEND TIME WITH YOUR CHILD**

There is a joke which you will hear while your child grows up. When you are asked how tall your child has grown up, you answer by gesture with your hands apart horizontally. This is because your child leaves for school early while you are asleep and the child is asleep when you come home late from office. Therefore you have not seen him grow tall vertically on office days. Suddenly you can see him how tall he is when you are at home on Sundays. 

However, do not take it as a simple joke. Once your child is 18 years old and pass out Class XII, he may be going out of station or abroad for higher studies and subsequently for good jobs anywhere on this earth. This means you may get to stay with your child at your home only for the first 18 years of his life and thereafter you will miss him except for a few days when the child will have time to meet you at home on his holidays.

At the same time, you will be very busy at office trying to secure your good future year after year. But amidst this rat race, do not ignore to spend as much time as possible with your child. Remember you may not get to see him for long after he reaches 18 years of age. Your good times spent with your child will remain etched in your mind forever. Do not miss these times. It will never come back again 

**CULTIVATE A HOBBY**

There will be times in your life when you are very sad. Maybe your boss did not give you a promotion which you thought of sure to get, or could be your fiancé decided for a break up, or incidents like these. You would not like to be besides of your friends or family at such sad moments. You would prefer to be alone for some while. So how do you spend these times alone? Cultivate a hobby which will keep you happy and busy to tide over your sad times or after your retirement. You can learn to play cards on your own; you can learn to play an instrument; you can learn to sing; you can learn to paint; or any other hobby that will give you a sense of fulfillment.

Music was my greatest de-stressor. Playing the harmonica and the guitar lit up my soul at sad times. These instruments are still a part of my life after retirement. 

**DEMONSTRATE UNBIASED PROFESIONALISM**

I have read it everywhere that even a peon would not like to wish or see your face on the street after you have retired as a Director or from a big position in the corporate. This may be true but not always. 

As a leader, if you had shown good leadership by demonstrating correct attitude, being kind and supportive, managing people humanly with care and respect, being grateful to people who supported you, encouraging people to do well and rewarding them for good work and your actions on employees and all stakeholders always being unbiased and transparent and to top it all if you had been successful in your organization, then people, whoever knew you and know you presently hearing from others do remember and respect you even after you retire or even die. 

Being unbiased even with your close colleagues and demonstrating your professionalism against personal bias at all times is seen as a great leadership. Such successful leaders are never forgotten.  

**ENJOY LIFE**

The Almighty have given you ONE life only. Even if your super rich, you have one life only. So always enjoy your life.

How many hours do you have in a year? 24 hours a day x365 days a year \= 8760 hours. Assume you sleep 8 hours a day; therefore you spend 8 x 365 hours \= 2920 hours. Assume you work 10 hours a day (except Sundays and 12 annual holidays) and commute 2 hours a day to office; therefore you spend 12 x (365-52-12) hours \= 3612 hours. So you have (8760-2920-3612) hours, ie. 2228 hours a year excluding time for work and sleep. 2228 hours out of 8760 hours means around 25% of your life; could be less if you work more. Have you ever imagined that you have only 25% of your life free of work and sleep which you can utilize for yourself, family and friends and relatives? If you consider 5% time gone for maintaining your house, household works, taking your child to school and tuitions, purchase of daily groceries, etc. then you are left with 20% of your life time only. Sounds pretty less\! But that is the fact of life. So enjoy your life well with this 20% of your time. It shall also rejuvenate you to carry on with your work at office. You can do so by allotting time for:

1) Savouring good food at home and restaurants  
2) Entertainment like hobbies, movies, fairs, gossiping with friends, etc  
3) Tours and Travels – local, out of station and international destinations

Sorry, if I have missed anything that you would like to enjoy life it. Whatever it is, remember what Mahatma Gandhi said \- “Live as if you were to die tomorrow. Learn as if you were to live forever”

**SEEING THROUGH DISRUPTORS**

Today you will come across many disrupters during your personal and corporate life. 

**Technological disruptors** will force you to change methods and techniques in doing your work and execute your project. As a simple example, when computers came in, typists were forced to learn software like Word, Excel, etc. to type on computers since the type writers were abolished. Any typist who did not do so lost his job.

**Geo-political disruptors** will force you to change the way you do business. As an example the tariff episodes between USA and many other countries including India is forcing many countries to look into other avenues for their business. Such disruptors play havoc on the stock markets with their volatility.

**Social disruptors** like break up of marriage, divorce, suicide, crimes, etc. will disturb your peace of mind which can affect your personal and corporate life. 

**Environmental disruptors** like pollution, food adulteration, climate change, etc. is taking a toll in our health.

In the spate of all these disruptors, you have to see through them with calm and pass them off because most are external inflicted where you have no control. It is easier said than done, but better if it can be done. You need to build a strong mind which is not easy to be disrupted. You must develop capabilities to control your mind and not allow your mind to take control of you.  

**QUIT IF UNHAPPY**

You can work in your company in two ways; one is with stress or pressure that is on you to perform and the other is with passion. 

When you work with passion, you forget how long you have been working on because you work with interest not by any external force or pressure to perform. When you work for long with stress and pressure, you will undergo hormonal changes in your body harming your health. Try to find a new suitable job where you can work happily before your health deteriorates. I know finding a new job is not easy, but go on trying to save your health. Quit.  

**CONTENTMENT IN LIFE**

Once a middle aged officer approached me in my cabin and informed that he would like to resign because of his low pay. I was astonished to hear about the low pay and called him along with the HR manager later on. On scrutiny of his pay slip, I found that his take home pay was very less but also observed that there were lot of deductions from his salary. On deeper scrutiny I found that there were several EMIs being deducted from his salary, one for new 3BHK property, one for SUV car, one for a racing bike and another for a 75 inch LED TV. I had to only convey that the company is providing you a reasonable salary but it is due to your heavy purchases that your take home salary is meager. 

Today many youngsters wants to have a property, car and a bike within latest 30 years of age, forget about other costly gadgets which they term them as necessary. There is no end to a materialistic world today. You must put a full stop to buying at some point of time. If you have a hatchback, you will want to have a sedan, then a SUV, then a Mercedes or a BMW, then a Ferrari, where is the end? There is no contentment in your life them, and therefore you will always remain unhappy. You need to balance your pocket. As I said earlier, you have to allot money wisely and reasonably for your savings, investment and your expenses, the total being what comes to your pocket.

If you develop the Attitude to use money judicially and be happy with whatever you have, you will achieve a state of contentment. Contentment is about being satisfied and happy with whatever you have in life. Contentment does not look at what you do not have in life. **Achieve a state of contentment; you will be happy in life**

**PART 30: SOME LESSONS I LEARNT**

**The Tongue**  
What is the most important organ of human life? Obviously the heart which pumps blood more than 100,000 times a day, which means if you live for 75 years, it will pump approx. 2.75 billion times. Most miraculously, it pumps on its own; you don’t do anything for it to pump, except for taking care of it sometimes with good food and aerobic exercise. With the blood being pumped by the heart, all other organs play their vital role in their own domains.

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Split original_draft.md into individual chapter files."""

import os
import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(text):
    """Convert text to a filesystem-friendly slug."""
    text = text.strip().lower()
    text = text.replace("&", "and")
    text = text.replace("'", "")
    text = text.replace('"', "")
    text = text.replace("'", "")
    text = text.replace("\u2019", "")
    text = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]", "", text)
    text = re.sub(r"[-\s]+", "_", text)
    text = text.strip("_")
    return text


def make_frontmatter(title, part, section, start_line, end_line):
    return (
        f"---\n"
        f'title: "{title}"\n'
        f"part: {part}\n"
        f"section: {section}\n"
        f'original_lines: "{start_line}-{end_line}"\n'
        f"---\n\n"
    )


def write_chapter(filepath, frontmatter, content_lines):
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(filepath), exist_ok=True)
    content = "\n".join(content_lines).strip()
    if not content:
        return
    with open(filepath, "w") as f:
        f.write(frontmatter)
        f.write(content)
        f.write("\n")


def detect_boundaries(lines):
    """Detect all structural boundaries in the manuscript."""
    boundaries = []  # (line_idx, type, raw_text)

    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        stripped = line.strip()

        # Section headers (in body, after TOC)
        if i >= 424 and re.match(r"\*\*SECTION \d", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "section", stripped))

        # Part headers (in body)
        if i >= 424 and re.match(r"\*\*PART \d", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "part", stripped))

        # Chapter headers
        if re.match(r"\*\*CHAPTER\s*:", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "chapter", stripped))

        # Annexure headers
        if re.match(r"\*\*ANNEXURE:", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "annexure", stripped))

        # Good practices sections
        if re.match(r"\*\*(SOME GOOD PRACTICES|Some good practices)\*\*", stripped):
            if i >= 424:
                boundaries.append((i, "good_practices", stripped))

    return boundaries


def extract_title_from_bold(text):
    """Extract title from **BOLD TEXT** pattern."""
    m = re.match(r"\*\*(.+?)\*\*", text.strip())
    if m:
        return m.group(1).strip()
    return text.strip().strip("*").strip()


def split_manuscript():
    with open("original_draft.md", "r") as f:
        raw = f.read()
    lines = raw.split("\n")
    total_lines = len(lines)

    index_entries = []

    # --- Frontmatter (lines 1-424) ---
    frontmatter_sections = [
        ("introduction", "Introduction to the Book", 345, 348),
        ("preface", "Preface", 351, 393),
        ("about_the_author", "About the Author", 395, 405),
        ("acknowledgement", "Acknowledgement", 407, 415),
        ("disclaimer", "Disclaimer", 417, 423),
    ]

    # Find actual line ranges by searching
    fm_markers = {
        "introduction": (r"\*\*INTRODUCTION TO THE BOOK\*\*", r"\*\*PREFACE\*\*"),
        "preface": (r"\*\*PREFACE\*\*", r"\*\*ABOUT THE AUTHOR\*\*"),
        "about_the_author": (r"\*\*ABOUT THE AUTHOR\*\*", r"\*\*ACKNOWLEDGEMENT\*\*"),
        "acknowledgement": (r"\*\*ACKNOWLEDGEMENT\*\*", r"\*\*DISCLAIMER\*\*"),
        "disclaimer": (r"\*\*DISCLAIMER\*\*", r"\*\*SECTION 1 – LEADERSHIP\*\*"),
    }

    # Find frontmatter boundaries in the body (after TOC, line ~345+)
    fm_positions = {}
    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        if i < 340:
            continue
        stripped = line.strip()
        if stripped == "**INTRODUCTION TO THE BOOK**":
            fm_positions["introduction_start"] = i
        elif stripped == "**PREFACE**":
            fm_positions["preface_start"] = i
        elif stripped == "**ABOUT THE AUTHOR**":
            fm_positions["about_the_author_start"] = i
        elif stripped == "**ACKNOWLEDGEMENT**":
            fm_positions["acknowledgement_start"] = i
        elif stripped == "**DISCLAIMER**":
            fm_positions["disclaimer_start"] = i
        elif i >= 420 and re.match(r"\*\*SECTION 1", stripped):
            fm_positions["section1_start"] = i
            break

    os.makedirs("chapters/00_frontmatter", exist_ok=True)

    fm_order = ["introduction", "preface", "about_the_author", "acknowledgement", "disclaimer"]
    fm_titles = {
        "introduction": "Introduction to the Book",
        "preface": "Preface",
        "about_the_author": "About the Author",
        "acknowledgement": "Acknowledgement",
        "disclaimer": "Disclaimer",
    }
    fm_ends = {
        "introduction": "preface_start",
        "preface": "about_the_author_start",
        "about_the_author": "acknowledgement_start",
        "acknowledgement": "disclaimer_start",
        "disclaimer": "section1_start",
    }

    for idx, key in enumerate(fm_order):
        start = fm_positions[f"{key}_start"]

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Split original_draft.md into individual chapter files."""

import os
import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(text):
    """Convert text to a filesystem-friendly slug."""
    text = text.strip().lower()
    text = text.replace("&", "and")
    text = text.replace("'", "")
    text = text.replace('"', "")
    text = text.replace("'", "")
    text = text.replace("\u2019", "")
    text = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]", "", text)
    text = re.sub(r"[-\s]+", "_", text)
    text = text.strip("_")
    return text


def make_frontmatter(title, part, section, start_line, end_line):
    return (
        f"---\n"
        f'title: "{title}"\n'
        f"part: {part}\n"
        f"section: {section}\n"
        f'original_lines: "{start_line}-{end_line}"\n'
        f"---\n\n"
    )


def write_chapter(filepath, frontmatter, content_lines):
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(filepath), exist_ok=True)
    content = "\n".join(content_lines).strip()
    if not content:
        return
    with open(filepath, "w") as f:
        f.write(frontmatter)
        f.write(content)
        f.write("\n")


def detect_boundaries(lines):
    """Detect all structural boundaries in the manuscript."""
    boundaries = []  # (line_idx, type, raw_text)

    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        stripped = line.strip()

        # Section headers (in body, after TOC)
        if i >= 424 and re.match(r"\*\*SECTION \d", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "section", stripped))

        # Part headers (in body)
        if i >= 424 and re.match(r"\*\*PART \d", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "part", stripped))

        # Chapter headers
        if re.match(r"\*\*CHAPTER\s*:", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "chapter", stripped))

        # Annexure headers
        if re.match(r"\*\*ANNEXURE:", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "annexure", stripped))

        # Good practices sections
        if re.match(r"\*\*(SOME GOOD PRACTICES|Some good practices)\*\*", stripped):
            if i >= 424:
                boundaries.append((i, "good_practices", stripped))

    return boundaries


def extract_title_from_bold(text):
    """Extract title from **BOLD TEXT** pattern."""
    m = re.match(r"\*\*(.+?)\*\*", text.strip())
    if m:
        return m.group(1).strip()
    return text.strip().strip("*").strip()

file.content:
---
title: "Introduction - My Learning"
part: 30
section: 5
original_lines: "4639-4648"
---

**The Tongue**  
What is the most important organ of human life? Obviously the heart which pumps blood more than 100,000 times a day, which means if you live for 75 years, it will pump approx. 2.75 billion times. Most miraculously, it pumps on its own; you don’t do anything for it to pump, except for taking care of it sometimes with good food and aerobic exercise. With the blood being pumped by the heart, all other organs play their vital role in their own domains.

But what is the most important organ for living a good life? I say “the tongue”. Tongue is the only organ for the sense of taste and unhealthy but tasty food destroys our health. Food is a vital factor of our health other than some other lifestyle habits. So our tongue is responsible for our physical health. 

Also we can speak because of our tongue. The way we speak determines our character and this character determines your relationship, be it with your family or friends or relatives or neighbours or colleagues in the corporate. The way politicians speak also determines his relationship with the public because politicians are public figures. So tongue is the main organ for your relationships. It is said that deaf and dumb do not have enemies; yes, they may have the tongue but cannot speak. 

**Lesson**: The tongue is responsible for two major aspects in one’s life; your physical health and your relationships. Watch your tongue from now on.  
\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Split original_draft.md into individual chapter files."""

import os
import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(text):
    """Convert text to a filesystem-friendly slug."""
    text = text.strip().lower()
    text = text.replace("&", "and")
    text = text.replace("'", "")
    text = text.replace('"', "")
    text = text.replace("'", "")
    text = text.replace("\u2019", "")
    text = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]", "", text)
    text = re.sub(r"[-\s]+", "_", text)
    text = text.strip("_")
    return text


def make_frontmatter(title, part, section, start_line, end_line):
    return (
        f"---\n"

                chapters_in_part.append((li, "annexure", title, abs_line))

                chapters_in_part.append((li, "good_practices", "Some Good Practices", abs_line))

                title = extract_title_from_bold(stripped)

                if len(title) > 3 and title not in ("ANNEXURES",):

                # Part 30 bold topic headers (mixed case)
                if re.match(r"\*\*[A-Z][a-zA-Z &,'-]+\*\*\s*$", stripped):

            elif pnum <= 26 and li > 2 and not re.match(r"\*\*(SOME GOOD|Some good|The role|CHAPTER|ANNEXURE|PART|SECTION)", stripped):

                    chapters_in_part.append((li, "chapter", title, abs_line))

            stripped = part_lines[li].strip()
            if re.match(r"\*\*PART \d", stripped):
                continue
            if not stripped:
                continue
            # Found first content line
            intro_start = li
            break

        # Write intro if there's content
        intro_content = part_lines[intro_start:intro_end]
        # Check if there's actual content (not just blank lines)
        has_intro_content = any(l.strip() for l in intro_content)
        if has_intro_content:
            intro_filepath = f"{part_dir}/00_intro.md"
            fm = make_frontmatter(
                f"Introduction - {part_title}",

            )

            rel_path = f"{sec_folder}/{part_folder_name}/00_intro.md"
            index_entries.append(f"- [{rel_path}]({rel_path}) - Introduction: {part_title}")

        for ci, (local_start, ctype, title, abs_start) in enumerate(chapters_in_part):
            if ci + 1 < len(chapters_in_part):
                local_end = chapters_in_part[ci + 1][0]
            else:
                local_end = len(part_lines)

            content = part_lines[local_start:local_end]

            # Generate filename
            file_idx = ci + 1
            slug = slugify(title)
            if len(slug) > 60:
                slug = slug[:60].rstrip("_")

            if ctype == "good_practices":
                filename = f"99_good_practices.md"
                # Check if there's already a 99_good_practices - use different number
                existing = [c for c in chapters_in_part[:ci] if c[2] == "Some Good Practices"]
                if existing:
                    filename = f"99b_good_practices.md"
            elif ctype == "annexure":
                filename = f"{file_idx:02d}_{slug}.md"
            else:
                filename = f"{file_idx:02d}_{slug}.md"

            filepath = f"{part_dir}/{filename}"
            fm = make_frontmatter(title, pnum, sec, abs_start + 1, pstart + local_end)
            write_chapter(filepath, fm, content)
            rel_path = f"{sec_folder}/{part_folder_name}/{filename}"
            prefix = "Annexure" if ctype == "annexure" else "Chapter"
            if ctype == "good_practices":
                prefix = ""
                display = "Some Good Practices"
            elif ctype == "topic":
                prefix = ""
                display = title
            else:
                display = title
            label = f"{prefix}: {display}" if prefix else display
            index_entries.append(f"- [{rel_path}]({rel_path}) - {label}")

    # Write index
    with open("chapters/index.md", "w") as f:
        f.write("# Book Index - Avoid Misgovernance in Corporates\n\n")
        f.write("## Reading Order\n\n")
        for entry in index_entries:
            f.write(entry + "\n")

    print(f"Split complete. {len(index_entries)} files written.")
    print("Index written to chapters/index.md")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    split_manuscript()

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Split original_draft.md into individual chapter files."""

import os
import re
import unicodedata


def slugify(text):
    """Convert text to a filesystem-friendly slug."""
    text = text.strip().lower()
    text = text.replace("&", "and")
    text = text.replace("'", "")
    text = text.replace('"', "")
    text = text.replace("'", "")
    text = text.replace("\u2019", "")
    text = re.sub(r"[^\w\s-]", "", text)
    text = re.sub(r"[-\s]+", "_", text)
    text = text.strip("_")
    return text


def make_frontmatter(title, part, section, start_line, end_line):
    return (
        f"---\n"
        f'title: "{title}"\n'
        f"part: {part}\n"
        f"section: {section}\n"
        f'original_lines: "{start_line}-{end_line}"\n'
        f"---\n\n"
    )


def write_chapter(filepath, frontmatter, content_lines):
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(filepath), exist_ok=True)
    content = "\n".join(content_lines).strip()
    if not content:
        return
    with open(filepath, "w") as f:
        f.write(frontmatter)
        f.write(content)
        f.write("\n")


def detect_boundaries(lines):
    """Detect all structural boundaries in the manuscript."""
    boundaries = []  # (line_idx, type, raw_text)

    for i, line in enumerate(lines):
        stripped = line.strip()

        # Section headers (in body, after TOC)
        if i >= 424 and re.match(r"\*\*SECTION \d", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "section", stripped))

        # Part headers (in body)
        if i >= 424 and re.match(r"\*\*PART \d", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "part", stripped))

        # Chapter headers
        if re.match(r"\*\*CHAPTER\s*:", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "chapter", stripped))

        # Annexure headers
        if re.match(r"\*\*ANNEXURE:", stripped):
            boundaries.append((i, "annexure", stripped))

        # Good practices sections
        if re.match(r"\*\*(SOME GOOD PRACTICES|Some good practices)\*\*", stripped):
            if i >= 424:
                boundaries.append((i, "good_practices", stripped))

    return boundaries
