                # Skip if it's just a one-line bold statement within a topic
                if len(title) > 3 and title not in ("ANNEXURES",):
                    chapters_in_part.append((li, "topic", title, abs_line))
            elif pnum == 30 and li > 2:
                # Part 30 bold topic headers (mixed case)
                if re.match(r"\*\*[A-Z][a-zA-Z &,'-]+\*\*\s*$", stripped):
                    title = extract_title_from_bold(stripped)
                    if len(title) > 3:
                        chapters_in_part.append((li, "topic", title, abs_line))
            # Non-chapter bold headers for force-your-style, deliberate-procrastination pattern
            elif pnum <= 26 and li > 2 and not re.match(r"\*\*(SOME GOOD|Some good|The role|CHAPTER|ANNEXURE|PART|SECTION)", stripped):
                if re.match(r'\*\*THE "[A-Z\s]+" SYNDROME\*\*', stripped, re.IGNORECASE):
                    title = extract_title_from_bold(stripped)
                    chapters_in_part.append((li, "chapter", title, abs_line))
                elif re.match(r"\*\*THE .+ SYNDROME\*\*", stripped, re.IGNORECASE):
                    title = extract_title_from_bold(stripped)
                    chapters_in_part.append((li, "chapter", title, abs_line))

        # Determine intro content (between part header and first chapter)
        intro_end = chapters_in_part[0][0] if chapters_in_part else len(part_lines)

        # Find where the actual intro content starts (skip part header lines)
        intro_start = 0
        for li in range(min(intro_end, len(part_lines))):
            stripped = part_lines[li].strip()
            if re.match(r"\*\*PART \d", stripped):
                continue
            if not stripped:
                continue
            # Found first content line
            intro_start = li
            break

        # Write intro if there's content
        intro_content = part_lines[intro_start:intro_end]
        # Check if there's actual content (not just blank lines)
        has_intro_content = any(l.strip() for l in intro_content)
        if has_intro_content:
            intro_filepath = f"{part_dir}/00_intro.md"
            fm = make_frontmatter(
                f"Introduction - {part_title}",
                pnum, sec,
                pstart + intro_start + 1,
                pstart + intro_end
            )
            write_chapter(intro_filepath, fm, intro_content)
            rel_path = f"{sec_folder}/{part_folder_name}/00_intro.md"
            index_entries.append(f"- [{rel_path}]({rel_path}) - Introduction: {part_title}")

        # Write each chapter/topic
        for ci, (local_start, ctype, title, abs_start) in enumerate(chapters_in_part):
            if ci + 1 < len(chapters_in_part):
                local_end = chapters_in_part[ci + 1][0]
            else:
                local_end = len(part_lines)

            content = part_lines[local_start:local_end]

            # Generate filename
            file_idx = ci + 1
            slug = slugify(title)
            if len(slug) > 60:
                slug = slug[:60].rstrip("_")

            if ctype == "good_practices":
                filename = f"99_good_practices.md"
                # Check if there's already a 99_good_practices - use different number
                existing = [c for c in chapters_in_part[:ci] if c[2] == "Some Good Practices"]
                if existing:
                    filename = f"99b_good_practices.md"
            elif ctype == "annexure":
                filename = f"{file_idx:02d}_{slug}.md"
            else:
                filename = f"{file_idx:02d}_{slug}.md"

            filepath = f"{part_dir}/{filename}"
            fm = make_frontmatter(title, pnum, sec, abs_start + 1, pstart + local_end)
            write_chapter(filepath, fm, content)
            rel_path = f"{sec_folder}/{part_folder_name}/{filename}"
            prefix = "Annexure" if ctype == "annexure" else "Chapter"
            if ctype == "good_practices":
                prefix = ""
                display = "Some Good Practices"
            elif ctype == "topic":
                prefix = ""
                display = title
            else:
                display = title
            label = f"{prefix}: {display}" if prefix else display
            index_entries.append(f"- [{rel_path}]({rel_path}) - {label}")

    # Write index
    with open("chapters/index.md", "w") as f:
        f.write("# Book Index - Avoid Misgovernance in Corporates\n\n")
        f.write("## Reading Order\n\n")
        for entry in index_entries:
            f.write(entry + "\n")

    print(f"Split complete. {len(index_entries)} files written.")
    print("Index written to chapters/index.md")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    split_manuscript()

input.command:
python3 -c "
import os, re

# Read original
with open('/Users/sagarpal/projects/baba_book/original_draft.md') as f:
    original = f.read()

# Read all chapter files in order from index.md
with open('/Users/sagarpal/projects/baba_book/chapters/index.md') as f:
    index_lines = f.readlines()

total_chapter_chars = 0
files_read = 0
for line in index_lines:
    m = re.search(r'\[([^\]]+)\]', line)
    if not m:
        continue
    path = m.group(1)
    filepath = os.path.join('/Users/sagarpal/projects/baba_book/chapters', path)
    if not os.path.exists(filepath):
        print(f'MISSING: {filepath}')
        continue
    with open(filepath) as f:
        content = f.read()
    # Strip frontmatter
    if content.startswith('---'):
        end = content.index('---', 3)
        content = content[end+3:].strip()
    total_chapter_chars += len(content)
    files_read += 1

# Count body chars in original (from line 345 onwards)
body_lines = original.split('\n')[344:]  # 0-indexed, so line 345
body_text = '\n'.join(body_lines).strip()
body_chars = len(body_text)

print(f'Files read: {files_read}')
print(f'Chapter content chars: {total_chapter_chars}')
print(f'Original body chars (from line 345): {body_chars}')
print(f'Ratio: {total_chapter_chars/body_chars:.2%}')
" 2>&1

# Edit Summary — Avoid Misgovernance in Corporates

This document summarises all proofreading and editorial changes made to the manuscript after splitting it into individual chapter files. Changes are organised by Section, Part, and Chapter.

**Editorial guidelines applied:**
- Indian English conventions (organisation, programme, honour, etc.)
- Spelling, grammar, punctuation, subject-verb agreement, tense consistency
- Preserved author's voice, anecdotes, domain terms, and personal style
- No content added or removed — only existing text improved

---

## Frontmatter

### 01_introduction.md (lines 345–348)

| Para 1 | Punctuation | "(CXOs) , Presidents" | "(CXOs), Presidents" | Extraneous space |
| Para 2 | Agreement | "chapters of the book discusses on" | "chapters of the book discuss" | Plural subject; unnecessary "on" |

| Para 8 | Grammar | "personal advices" | "personal advice" | Uncountable noun |
| Notes | Punctuation | "her's" | "hers" | No apostrophe in possessive pronoun |

### 03_about_the_author.md (lines 395–405)

---

**08_image_nisation.md** — Fixed "bad names it" → "badmouth it", "administered courage" → "mustered courage", "program" → "programme".

**09_no_heed_to_cost_and_quality.md** — Fixed "program" → "programme" (5×), preposition corrections, tense consistency.

**10_the_unethical_money.md** — Fixed "Vigilant Department" → "Vigilance Department", "victimized" → "victimised".

**11_disrespect_and_ungratefulness.md** — Fixed "saved a catastrophic fire" → "averted", "money do bring" → "does bring", "World cup" → "World Cup".

**99_good_practices.md** — Fixed pronoun consistency, "programs" → "programmes", "organization" → "organisation".

---

## Section 3 — Operations

**Part 3** — Fixed "comprises of" → "comprises" (multiple), "organization" → "organisation" throughout, "utilizing" → "utilising", missing commas after conjunctive adverbs, "comprising of" → "comprising" (5×), subject-verb agreements.

**Part 9** — Fixed "vindicated" → "victimised", "stealing stationary" → "stationery", "organization lose" → "loses", "kilometer" → "kilometre", preposition corrections.

content.text:
All steps are complete. Here's a summary:

**Step 1-2: Split & Commit** — Created `split_manuscript.py` that parsed the 4,721-line manuscript into **214 individual markdown files** across a two-level directory structure (`chapters/`), with YAML frontmatter and an `index.md` manifest. 99.81% content preservation verified. Committed.

**Step 3: Proofread** — Dispatched 10 parallel sub-agents covering all 30 parts + frontmatter. **208 files** were edited with fixes including:
- Indian English spelling (organisation, programme, etc.)
- Grammar (subject-verb agreement, tense consistency)
- Spelling corrections (typos like "demine"→"demean", "stationary"→"stationery")
- Punctuation and phrasing improvements
- Domain-specific fixes (e.g., "quality"→"safety" in Safety chapter where copied from Quality)

**Step 4: Edit Summary** — Created `EDIT_SUMMARY.md` documenting all changes by Section → Part → Chapter.

**Step 5: PDF Script** — Created `generate_pdf.py` (using `markdown2` + `weasyprint`) and `pyproject.toml` with uv-compatible dependencies.

**Step 6: Commits** — Three commits made:
1. `67bc590` — Split manuscript into chapter files
2. `9c6f498` — Proofread and edit all chapters
3. `2159a7f` — Add PDF conversion script

# Edit Summary — Avoid Misgovernance in Corporates

This document summarises all proofreading and editorial changes made to the manuscript after splitting it into individual chapter files. Changes are organised by Section, Part, and Chapter.

**Editorial guidelines applied:**
- Indian English conventions (organisation, programme, honour, etc.)
- Spelling, grammar, punctuation, subject-verb agreement, tense consistency
- Preserved author's voice, anecdotes, domain terms, and personal style
- No content added or removed — only existing text improved

---

## Frontmatter

### 01_introduction.md (lines 345–348)
| Location | Type | Original | Edited | Reason |
|----------|------|----------|--------|--------|
| Para 1 | Grammar | "interest to read" | "interest in reading" | Correct preposition usage |
| Para 1 | Punctuation | "With all honesty I say" | "With all honesty, I say" | Missing comma |
| Para 1 | Grammar | "leaders of future" | "leaders of the future" | Missing article |
| Para 1 | Punctuation | "DGM) , General Managers" | "DGM), General Managers" | Extraneous space |
| Para 1 | Punctuation | "(CXOs) , Presidents" | "(CXOs), Presidents" | Extraneous space |
| Para 2 | Agreement | "chapters of the book discusses on" | "chapters of the book discuss" | Plural subject; unnecessary "on" |

### 02_preface.md (lines 351–393)

|----------|------|----------|--------|--------|
| Multiple | Spelling | "organization" (7×) | "organisation" | Indian English |

| Para 1 | Grammar | "may not be always appropriate" | "may not always be appropriate" | Adverb placement |
| Para 4 | Spelling | "demine" | "demean" | Misspelling |
| Para 5 | Grammar | "problems aroused" | "problems that arose" | Wrong word |
| Para 5 | Agreement | "construction projects encompasses" | "construction projects encompass" | Plural subject |
| Para 6 | Grammar | "draw your attention on" | "draw your attention to" | Preposition |
| Para 8 | Agreement | "Annexures...illustrates" | "Annexures...illustrate" | Plural subject |
| Para 8 | Grammar | "personal advices" | "personal advice" | Uncountable noun |
| Notes | Punctuation | "her's" | "hers" | No apostrophe in possessive pronoun |

### 03_about_the_author.md (lines 395–405)
| Location | Type | Original | Edited | Reason |
|----------|------|----------|--------|--------|

| Para 3 | Grammar | "After a eight year stint" | "After an eight-year stint" | Article; hyphenation |

### 05_disclaimer.md (lines 417–423)

## Section 1 — Leadership

**08_image_nisation.md** — Fixed "bad names it" → "badmouth it", "administered courage" → "mustered courage", "program" → "programme".

**99_good_practices.md** — Fixed pronoun consistency, "programs" → "programmes", "organization" → "organisation".
